documentation.value: Outputs one or more strings. Not actually a function but a language construct; parentheses are optional.
One or more string expressions to output.
Dumps information about one or more variables, including type and value. Useful for debugging.
One or more variables to dump. Outputs type, length, and values recursively.
Prints human-readable information about a variable. When $return is true, returns the output as a string.
The variable to print.
If true, returns the output as a string instead of printing it.
Applies a callback function to each element of an array and returns a new array with the results.
The callback function to apply. Receives array values as arguments. NULL creates an array of arrays.
The input array to map over.
Optional additional arrays. If passed, the callback receives corresponding elements from each.
Filters elements of an array using a callback function. If no callback is supplied, removes falsy values.
The array to filter.
The callback function. Return true to keep, false to remove.
Flag: ARRAY_FILTER_USE_KEY (pass key), ARRAY_FILTER_USE_BOTH (pass value and key).
Merges one or more arrays. Values with numeric keys are renumbered; string keys are overwritten by later arrays.
The arrays to merge.
Pushes one or more elements onto the end of an array. Returns the new number of elements.
The input array (passed by reference).
The values to push.
Returns the length of a string in bytes.
The string to measure.
Finds the position of the first occurrence of a substring. Returns false if not found.
The string to search in.
The substring to search for.
Optional starting position for the search.

signatures.label: str_replace(array|string $search, array|string $replace, array|string $subject, int &$count = null): array|string

documentation.value: Replaces all occurrences of the search string with the replacement string.
The value to search for (string or array of strings).
The replacement value (string or array of strings).
The string or array to perform replacements on.
Optional. Set to the number of replacements performed.

signatures.label: preg_match(string $pattern, string $subject, array &$matches = null, int $flags = 0, int $offset = 0): int|false

documentation.value: Performs a regular expression match. Returns 1 if matched, 0 if not, false on error.
The regex pattern to search for (e.g., '/\d+/').
The input string to match against.
Optional. Filled with the results of the match.
Optional flags: PREG_OFFSET_CAPTURE, PREG_UNMATCHED_AS_NULL.
Optional offset in bytes to start the search.
Returns the JSON representation of a value.
The value to encode. Can be any type except a resource.
Bitmask of JSON_* constants (e.g., JSON_PRETTY_PRINT, JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE).
Maximum nesting depth. Must be greater than zero.
Decodes a JSON string into a PHP value.
The JSON string to decode.
When true, JSON objects are returned as associative arrays instead of objects.
Maximum nesting depth of the structure being decoded.
Bitmask of JSON_* constants (e.g., JSON_BIGINT_AS_STRING, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR).

signatures.label: file_get_contents(string $filename, bool $use_include_path = false, ?resource $context = null, int $offset = 0, ?int $length = null): string|false

documentation.value: Reads the entire contents of a file into a string. Also supports reading from URLs via stream wrappers.
The file path or URL to read.
Whether to search the include_path for the file.
A stream context resource created with stream_context_create().
The offset to start reading from in the original stream.
Maximum number of bytes to read.
Formats a Unix timestamp (or the current time) according to the given format string.
The date format string (e.g., 'Y-m-d H:i:s', 'D, d M Y').
Optional Unix timestamp. Defaults to the current time.
